free_text: Study on production of 103Pd and characterisation of
possible contaminants in the proton irradiation of
Inst. of Nucl. Res. Debrecen, Hungary
CGR 560 cyclotron of the VUB, Belgium
High purity, (>99%) natRh foils of 26 microns from
Monitor foils: high purity (>99%)  Ti, Ni and Cu from
used. Seven stacks were irradiated with 14.7, 22.5 and
29.4  MeV proton beam. Beam current was monitored and
measured in Faraday-cup. Irradiation lasted from 1 hour
To two hours with 200-500 nA beam current.
from Table of radioactive isotopes,p.123-3, Wiley,
Extended range high purity Ge gamma detector
Low energy high purity Ge gamma and X-ray det.
The uncertainty of the primary beam energy was
+-0.3 MeV. The energy attenuation was calculated by
using the polynomial approximation of Andersen and
Ziegler along the stacks. The mean energy uncertainty
varies from +-0.4 to +-1.6 MeV along the stacks.
Total error of the data was about 10% to 16%.
